axioms.name: The Three Things Rule
axioms.statement: There should be at least three things going on in one scene at the same time. Otherwise it's just a conversation about a bank loan.

axioms.name: Reality Through Obstacles
axioms.statement: The more you put on a person, the more real it feels. Real conversations rarely happen in neutral conditions.

axioms.name: Show Through Navigation
axioms.statement: Obstacles force showing rather than telling—character is revealed through how they navigate complications, not what they declare.

axioms.name: Disarm Then Punch
axioms.statement: Disarm the audience with comedy, then punch them in the gut with drama when they least expect it.

core_algorithms.name: The Three Things Rule
core_algorithms.purpose: Ensure minimum dramatic density by layering simultaneous obstacles
core_algorithms.pseudocode: FOR scene IN script: obstacles = extract_obstacles(scene); distinct = deduplicate(obstacles); IF count(distinct) < 3: RETURN DRAMATICALLY_INERT; ELSE: RETURN HAS_REALITY

core_algorithms.name: Disarm-Then-Punch Protocol
core_algorithms.purpose: Deliver emotional impact by lowering audience defenses with comedy
core_algorithms.pseudocode: 1. Introduce complication -> GENERATE LAUGHTER (Guard Lowered); 2. Maintain comic rhythm; 3. SUDDENLY deliver emotional payload (Punch); 4. TIMING: When least expected;

core_algorithms.name: Reality Generation Principle
core_algorithms.purpose: Create authenticity by violating neutral conditions
core_algorithms.pseudocode: neutral_state = identify_neutral_conditions(scene); violations = select_violations(physical, bodily, temporal); APPLY violations TO scene; FORCE character TO navigate_while_pursuing_goal;

core_algorithms.name: Fourth Wall Breaking (Direct Address)
core_algorithms.purpose: Establish intimacy and complicity with the audience
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF character feels misunderstood/isolated: TURN to camera; SHARE subtext/judgment; ESTABLISH complicity; EFFECT: Audience becomes confidant/co-conspirator;

diagnostic_questions.question: Are there at least three distinct things going on in this scene simultaneously?
Could this scene happen in neutral conditions?
Is the character revealed through how they navigate obstacles?
Does comedy disarm the audience before a dramatic punch?
Is the environment actively making the character's goal harder?
Do obstacles interact and multiply each other?

key_constraints: Real conversations never happen in neutral conditions
Comedy opens the door for drama
